description:
You are a Linux systems engineer reviewing authentication logs as part of a hardening initiative.  
A sample syslog-style file already exists at:

/home/user/log_samples/auth.log

Your job is to isolate all high-risk authentication events and store them in a separate report that will later be shipped to a SIEM.

1. Create a new directory called /home/user/sec_reports (if it does not already exist).

2. Using a single **regular-expression based filter**, extract every line from /home/user/log_samples/auth.log that matches ANY of these case-insensitive phrases exactly:
   • “Failed password”  
   • “Invalid user”  
   • “authentication failure”  

   The phrases may appear anywhere in the line and the match must be case-insensitive.

3. Preserve the **original chronological order** of the matched lines.  
   Do **not** include any unmatched lines.

4. Save the result to the file:

/home/user/sec_reports/filtered_auth.log

5. The file /home/user/sec_reports/filtered_auth.log must have **exactly** the following format so that automated grading can check it:

   a) Line 1: the literal text  
      === SECURITY ERRORS BEGIN ===  

   b) Line 2 … N: only the log lines that matched the regex, in the same order they appeared in the source file.  
      Do not add or remove whitespace from those log lines.

   c) Last line: the literal text  
      === SECURITY ERRORS END ===  

   There must be a final newline character (ASCII 0x0A) after the terminating “END” marker.

6. When you finish, display on the terminal the exact phrase (without quotes):

   Report written to /home/user/sec_reports/filtered_auth.log

This task should be solvable with 5-10 shell commands (mkdir, grep/sed/awk, redirects, etc.).  Automated tests will verify:

• The directory /home/user/sec_reports exists.  
• The file /home/user/sec_reports/filtered_auth.log exists.  
• Its first and last lines match the required begin/end markers.  
• Every interior line is one that originally appeared in /home/user/log_samples/auth.log and contains at least one of the three target phrases.  
• No unmatched lines are present.  
• The console output includes the exact phrase specified in step 6.

Do **not** delete or modify any other files.  You do not have root privileges.  Begin when ready.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/log_samples/auth.log
